name: npc-session

metadata.name: npc_session
metadata.summary: Basic tools for parsing subject, session, date and time associated with data from the Mindscope Neuropixels team, in the cloud.

documentation:
# npc_session

**n**euro**p**ixels **c**loud **session**

Basic tools for parsing subject, session, date and time associated with data from the
Mindscope Neuropixels team, in the cloud.

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/npc-session.svg?label=PyPI&color=blue)](https://pypi.org/project/npc-session/)
[![Python version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/npc-session)](https://pypi.org/project/npc-session/)

[![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/alleninstitute/npc_session?logo=codecov)](https://app.codecov.io/github/AllenInstitute/npc_session)
[![CI/CD](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/alleninstitute/npc_session/publish.yml?label=CI/CD&logo=github)](https://github.com/alleninstitute/npc_session/actions/workflows/publish.yml)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/alleninstitute/npc_session?logo=github)](https://github.com/alleninstitute/npc_session/issues)


## quickstart

```bash
pip install npc_session
```

Parse a normalized ID from a path or string:
```python
>>> from npc_session import SessionRecord;

>>> s = SessionRecord('//allen/programs/mindscope/workgroups/templeton/TTOC/2022-07-26_14-09-36_366122')
>>> s
'366122_2022-07-26'
>>> s.idx
0
>>> s.subject
366122
>>> s.date
'2022-07-26'
>>> s.date.dt
datetime.date(2022, 7, 26)
>>> s.date.year
2022
>>> s_1 = SessionRecord('//allen/programs/mindscope/workgroups/templeton/TTOC/2022-07-26_14-09-36_366122_1')
>>> s_1.idx
1
>>> s_2 = s_1.with_idx(2)
>>> s_2.idx
2

```

modules.name: npc_session.jobs
documentation:
Protocols (interfaces) that define the required methods for jobs and
job-queues, and types for static analysis (mypy).

variables.name: npc_session.jobs.JobArgs
documentation: Types of value that can be stored - mainly for compatibility with redis/sqlite3 types.

variables.name: npc_session.jobs.JobKwargs
documentation:
Key:value pairs of job attributes.
For a job stored in sqlite, these would correspond to column-name:value pairs.

variables.name: npc_session.jobs.JobT
documentation: TypeVar with upper-bound `Job`.

variables.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueueT
documentation: TypeVar with upper-bound `JobQueue`.

classes.name: npc_session.jobs.Job

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.Job.__init__
documentation: Create a new job.

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.Job.session
documentation:
Session folder name, from which we can make an `np_session.Session` instance.

- each job must have a Session
- each queue can only have one job per session (session is unique)

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.Job.priority
documentation:
Priority level for this job.
Processed in descending order (then ordered by `added`).

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.Job.added
documentation:
When the job was added to the queue.
Jobs processed in ascending order (after ordering by `priority`).

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.Job.started
documentation: Whether the job has started (can also represent time).

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.Job.hostname
documentation:
The hostname of the machine that is currently processing this
session.

Can also be set to choose a specific machine to process the job.

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.Job.finished
documentation:
Whether the session has been verified as finished (can also
represent time).

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.Job.error
documentation: Error message, if the job errored.

Base class for jobs. The only required attribute is `session`, to
match a job with a session. All other fields can be set to None.

classes.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueue

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueue.__setitem__
documentation: Add a job to the queue.

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueue.__getitem__
documentation: Get a job from the queue.

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueue.__delitem__
documentation: Remove a job from the queue.

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueue.__contains__
documentation: Whether the session is in the queue.

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueue.__len__
documentation: Number of jobs in the queue.

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueue.__iter__
documentation: Iterate over all jobs in the queue.

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueue.__next__
documentation:
Get the next job to process.
Sorted by priority (desc), then date added (asc).

methods.name: npc_session.jobs.JobQueue.update
documentation: Update the fields on an existing entry.

Base class for job queues.

Implementations should subclass `collections.abc.MutableMapping`
to get methods like items, keys, get, setdefault, etc.

modules.name: npc_session.parsing
documentation:
Functions for extracting session-related components from strings.

Difficult to debug or modify without the fantastic https://regex101.com/

functions.name: npc_session.parsing._strip_non_numeric
documentation:
Remove all non-numeric characters from a string.

```pycon
>>> _strip_non_numeric('2021-06-01_10:12/34.0')
'202106011012340'
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_probe_letter
documentation:
```pycon
>>> extract_probe_letter('366122_2021-06-01_10:12:03_3_probe-A')
'A'
>>> extract_probe_letter('probeA')
'A'
>>> extract_probe_letter('A')
'A'
>>> extract_probe_letter('testB Probe A2 sessionC')
'A'
>>> None is extract_probe_letter('366122_2021-06-01_10:12:03_3')
True
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_isoformat_datetime
documentation:
Extract and normalize datetime from a string.
Return None if no datetime found.

```pycon
>>> extract_isoformat_datetime('2021-06-01_10:12:03')
'2021-06-01 10:12:03'
>>> extract_isoformat_datetime('20210601_101203')
'2021-06-01 10:12:03'
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_isoformat_date
documentation:
Extract and normalize date from a string.
Return None if no date found.

```pycon
>>> extract_isoformat_date('2021-06-01_10-00-00')
'2021-06-01'
>>> extract_isoformat_date('20210601_100000')
'2021-06-01'
>>> extract_isoformat_date(20210601)
'2021-06-01'
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_isoformat_time
documentation:
Extract and normalize time from a string.
Return None if no time found.

```pycon
>>> extract_isoformat_time('2021-06-01_10:12:03')
'10:12:03'
>>> extract_isoformat_time('20210601_101203')
'10:12:03'
>>> extract_isoformat_time('101203')
'10:12:03'
>>> extract_isoformat_time('20209900_251203') == None
True
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_subject
documentation:
Extract subject ID from a string.
Return None if no subject ID found.

```pycon
>>> extract_subject('366122_2021-06-01_10:12:03_3')
366122
>>> extract_subject('366122_20210601_101203')
366122
>>> extract_subject('366122_20210601')
366122
>>> extract_subject('0123456789')
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_session_index
documentation:
Extract appended session index from a string.

```pycon
>>> extract_session_index('366122_2021-06-01_10:12:03_3')
3
>>> extract_session_index('366122_2021-06-01')
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_session_id
documentation:
Extract session ID from a string.
Raises ValueError if no session ID found.

```pycon
>>> extract_session_id('2021-06-01_10:12:03_366122')
'366122_2021-06-01'
>>> extract_session_id('366122_20210601_101203')
'366122_2021-06-01'
>>> extract_session_id('366122_20210601_1')
'366122_2021-06-01_1'
>>> extract_session_id('DRPilot_366122_20210601')
'366122_2021-06-01'
>>> extract_session_id('3661_12345678_1')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: Could not extract date and subject from 3661_12345678_1
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_aind_session_id
documentation:
Extract session ID with AIND formatting.

Raises ValueError if no ID found.

```pycon
>>> extract_aind_session_id('ecephys_366122_2021-06-01_10-12-03_sorted')
'ecephys_366122_2021-06-01_10-12-03'
>>> extract_aind_session_id('prefix_ecephys_366122_2021-06-01_10-12-03_sorted')
'ecephys_366122_2021-06-01_10-12-03'
>>> extract_aind_session_id('ecephys_686740_2023_10_26_12_29_08_to_dlc_side')
'ecephys_686740_2023-10-26_12-29-08'
>>> extract_aind_session_id('data/ecephys_686740_2023_10_26_12_29_08_to_dlc_side')
'ecephys_686740_2023-10-26_12-29-08'
>>> extract_aind_session_id('data\ecephys_686740_2023_10_26_12_29_08_to_dlc_side')
'ecephys_686740_2023-10-26_12-29-08'
>>> extract_aind_session_id('366122_2021-06-01_10-12-03_sorted')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: Could not extract AIND session ID from 366122_2021-06-01_10-12-03_sorted
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_mvr_camera_name
documentation:
Extract MVR camera name from a string.
Raises ValueError if no camera name found.

```pycon
>>> extract_mvr_camera_name('Eye_20231023T141119.mp4')
'eye'
>>> extract_mvr_camera_name('Behavior_20231023T141119.mp4')
'behavior'
>>> extract_mvr_camera_name('1332563048_717439_20240222.beh.mp4')
'behavior'
>>> extract_mvr_camera_name('MVR_366122_2021-06-01_10-12-03')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: Could not extract MVR camera name from MVR_366122_2021-06-01_10-12-03
```

functions.name: npc_session.parsing.extract_rig_id
documentation:
Extracts a standardized rig id from a string.

More documentation on rig id parts at: `npc_session.records.RigIdRecord`

# Notes

- Parses amd extracts but does not validation, please refer to

> `npc_session.records.RigIdRecord` for that.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> extract_rig_id("BEH.D-Box2")
'BEHNSB.D-2'
>>> extract_rig_id("BEH.B")
'BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN'
>>> extract_rig_id("D2")
'BEHNSB.D-2'
>>> extract_rig_id("NP.0")
'NP.0'
>>> extract_rig_id("NP0")
'NP.0'
>>> extract_rig_id("BEHNSB.D-1")
'BEHNSB.D-1'
>>> extract_rig_id("342_BEHDEV.B-2_240401")
'BEHDEV.B-2'
>>> extract_rig_id("342_BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN_240401")
'BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN'
>>> extract_rig_id("UNKNOWN_BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN")
'BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN'
>>> extract_rig_id("unknown_BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN")
'BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN'
>>> extract_rig_id("unknown_BEHDEV.B-unknown")
'BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN'
```

variables.name: npc_session.parsing.PARSE_SESSION_ID
documentation: does not allow time after date

variables.name: npc_session.parsing.RIG_ID_ROOM_NUMBER
documentation: range incase room number is less than 3 digits

variables.name: npc_session.parsing.RIG_ID_MAJOR
documentation: all current prefixes are 3 characters but this allows for future expansion

classes.name: npc_session.records.MetadataRecord

methods.name: npc_session.records.MetadataRecord.parse_id
documentation: Pre-validation. Handle any parsing or casting to get to the stored type.

methods.name: npc_session.records.MetadataRecord.validate_id
documentation: Post-parsing. Raise ValueError if not a valid value for this type of metadata record.

methods.name: npc_session.records.MetadataRecord.id
documentation:
A unique identifier for the object.
Read-only, and type at assignment is preserved.

methods.name: npc_session.records.MetadataRecord.id
documentation:
A unique identifier for the object.
Write-once, then read-only. Type at assignment is preserved.

A base class for definitions of unique metadata records
(unique amongst that type of metadata, not necessarily globally).

Intended use is for looking-up and referring to metadata components
in databases, regardless of the backend.

Each implementation should:
\- accept a variety of input formats
\- parse, normalize, then validate input
\- give a single consistent representation (`id`)
\- store `id` as an int or str, "immutable" after init

Instances are effectively the same as their stored `id` attribute, but with extra
properties, including magic methods for str, equality, hashing and ordering:

```pycon
>>> a = MetadataRecord(1)
>>> a
1
>>> str(a)
'1'
>>> a == 1 and a == '1'
True
>>> isinstance(a, (int, str))
False
>>> b = MetadataRecord(1)
>>> c = MetadataRecord(2)
>>> a == b
True
>>> a == c
False
>>> sorted([c, b])
[1, 2]
>>> a == MetadataRecord(a)
True
>>> MetadataRecord(1).id = 2
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
AttributeError: MetadataRecord.id is read-only
```

classes.name: npc_session.records.ProjectRecord
documentation:
To uniquely define a project we need:
\- `id`: a descriptive string or acronym

```pycon
>>> project = ProjectRecord('DR')
>>> project
'DR'
>>> project == 'DR'
True
>>> isinstance(project, str)
True
```

classes.name: npc_session.records.SubjectRecord
documentation:
To uniquely define a subject we need:
\- `id`: labtracks MID

```pycon
>>> subject = SubjectRecord('366122')
>>> subject = SubjectRecord(366122)
>>> subject
366122
>>> subject == 366122 and subject == '366122'
True
>>> isinstance(subject, int)
True
>>> hash(subject) == hash(366122)
True
```

classes.name: npc_session.records.ProbeRecord
documentation:
Probe records stored as A-F

```pycon
>>> ProbeRecord('A')
'A'
>>> ProbeRecord('A').name
'probeA'
>>> ProbeRecord('testB Probe A2 sessionC')
'A'
>>> ProbeRecord('366122_2021-06-01_10:12:03_3')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: ProbeRecord requires a string containing `probe` followed by a letter A-F, not '366122_2021-06-01_10:12:03_3'
>>> 'A' == ProbeRecord('probeA')
True
>>> 'probeA' == ProbeRecord('probeA')
False
>>> 'A' in {ProbeRecord('probeA')}
True
>>> ProbeRecord('probeA') in {'A'}
True
```

classes.name: npc_session.records.DateRecord

methods.name: npc_session.records.DateRecord.validate_id
documentation:
```pycon
>>> DateRecord.validate_id('2002-04-25')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: Invalid year: 2002
```

class_variables.name: npc_session.records.DateRecord.valid_id_regex
documentation: A valid date this century, format YYYY-MM-DD.

Date records are stored in isoformat with hyphen seperators.

```pycon
>>> DateRecord('2022-04-25')
'2022-04-25'
>>> DateRecord('20220425')
'2022-04-25'
>>> date = DateRecord('20220425')
>>> datetime.date.fromisoformat(str(date))
datetime.date(2022, 4, 25)
```

Components of date are also made available:
\>>> date.year, date.month, date.day
(2022, 4, 25)

classes.name: npc_session.records.DatetimeRecord

class_variables.name: npc_session.records.DatetimeRecord.valid_id_regex
documentation: A valid datetime this century, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

Datetime records are stored in isoformat with a resolution of seconds,
and space separator between date/time, hyphen between date components, colon
between time components.

```pycon
>>> dt = DatetimeRecord('2022-04-25 15:02:37')
>>> dt = DatetimeRecord('366122_20220425_150237_2')
>>> dt
'2022-04-25 15:02:37'
>>> str(dt)
'2022-04-25 15:02:37'
>>> dt.dt
datetime.datetime(2022, 4, 25, 15, 2, 37)
```

Components of datetime are also made available:
\>>> dt.year, dt.month, dt.day, dt.hour, dt.minute, dt.second
(2022, 4, 25, 15, 2, 37)

If a date alone is provided, the time will default to 00:00:00:
\>>> DatetimeRecord('2022-04-25')
'2022-04-25 00:00:00'

classes.name: npc_session.records.TimeRecord

methods.name: npc_session.records.TimeRecord.validate_id
documentation:
```pycon
>>> TimeRecord.validate_id('15:02:37')
>>> TimeRecord.validate_id('15:02:37.123456')
>>> TimeRecord.validate_id('25:02:37.123456')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: Invalid time: 25:02:37.123
```

class_variables.name: npc_session.records.TimeRecord.valid_id_regex
documentation: A valid time, format HH:MM:SS

Time records are stored in isoformat with a resolution of seconds.

```pycon
>>> TimeRecord('15:02:37')
'15:02:37'
>>> TimeRecord('15:02:37.123') == TimeRecord(150237)
True
>>> TimeRecord('25:02:37')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: Invalid time: 25:02:37
```

classes.name: npc_session.records.SessionRecord

methods.name: npc_session.records.SessionRecord.with_idx
documentation:
Return a new instance with specified idx.

```pycon
>>> SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25_0').with_idx(1)
'366122_2022-04-25_1'
>>> SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25').with_idx(1)
'366122_2022-04-25_1'
```

If `idx` is unchanged, self is returned:
\>>> SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25').with_idx(0)
'366122_2022-04-25'
\>>> SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25_1').with_idx(1) # no change
'366122_2022-04-25_1'

class_variables.name: npc_session.records.SessionRecord.display_null_idx
documentation:
Whether to show `_0` when a session index is 0.
False as long as each subject has only one session per day.

To uniquely define a subject we need:
\- `id`: a multipart underscore-separated str corresponding to:

> \<subject_id>\_\<date>\_\<idx \[optional\]>
>
> where:
> \- `date` is in format YYYY-MM-DD, with optional separators (hyphen, slash)
> \- `idx` corresponds to the index of the session on that date for that
> subject
> \- `idx` must be appended as the last component, separated by an underscore
> \- if the last component is missing, it is implicitly assumed to be the only
> session on the date for that subject
> \- `idx` currently isn't needed, but is included for future-proofing
> \- varname `idx` is used to avoid conflicts with `index` methods in
> possible base classes

Record provides:
\- normalized id
\- subject record
\- date record
\- idx (0 if not specified)

We can extract these components from typical session identifiers, such as
folder names, regardless of component order:
\>>> a = SessionRecord('DRPilot_366122_20220425')
\>>> b = SessionRecord('0123456789_366122_20220425')
\>>> c = SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25')
\>>> d = SessionRecord('2022-04-25_12:00:00_366122')
\>>> a
'366122_2022-04-25'
\>>> a == b == c == d
True

Components are also available for use:
\>>> a.subject, a.date, a.idx
(366122, '2022-04-25', 0)
\>>> a.date.year, a.date.month, a.date.day
(2022, 4, 25)

Missing index is synonymous with idx=0. The default behavior
is to hide a null index, since they're not currently used:
\>>> SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25').idx
0
\>>> SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25_1').idx
1

`idx`, like other properties, is read-only.
Use `with_idx` to create a new instance:
\>>> a = SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25_1')
\>>> a.with_idx(2)
'366122_2022-04-25_2'
\>>> a is not a.with_idx(2)
True

Subject and date are validated on init:
\- subject must be a recent or near-future labtracks MID:
\>>> SessionRecord('1_2022-04-25')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: SessionRecord.id must be in format \<subject_id>\_\<date>\_\<idx \[optional\]>

- date must be a valid recent or near-future date:

```pycon
>>> SessionRecord('366122_2022-13-25')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: SessionRecord.id must be in format <subject_id>_<date>_<idx
[optional]>
```

Comparisons are based on the session's normalized id:
\>>> assert SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25_0') == '366122_2022-04-25'
\>>> assert SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25') == '366122_2022-04-25_0'
\>>> a = {SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25_0')}

Validator can also be used to check if a string is a valid session id:
\>>> SessionRecord.validate_id('366122_2022-04-25_0') == None
True
\>>> SessionRecord.validate_id('366122_2022-99-25_0') == None
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: SessionRecord.id must match SessionRecord.valid_id_regex

```pycon
>>> a = SessionRecord('DRPilot_366122_20220425')
>>> assert a[:] in a.id, f"slicing should access {a.id[:]=} , not the original value passed to init {a[:]=}"
```

\# hashes match string with idx
\>>> assert '366122_2022-04-25_0' in {SessionRecord('366122_2022-04-25')}

classes.name: npc_session.records.AINDSessionRecord

methods.name: npc_session.records.AINDSessionRecord.__eq__
documentation:
```pycon
>>> assert AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') == AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35')
>>> assert AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') == AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35_sorted_2023-12-16_06-52-11')
>>> assert AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') == 'ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35'
```

```pycon
>>> assert AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') != 'ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-34'
```

Comparison possible with stringable types:
\>>> assert AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') == MetadataRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35')

methods.name: npc_session.records.AINDSessionRecord.__hash__

documentation:
To uniquely define an AIND session we need:
\- `id`: a multipart underscore-separated str corresponding to:

> \<platform>\_\<subject_id>\_\<date>\_\<time>
>
> where:
> \- `platform` is text, with possible hyphens
> \- `subject_id` is a labtracks MID (digits)
> \- `date` is in format YYYY-MM-DD, with hyphen separators
> \- `time` is in format HH:MM:SS, with hyphen separators

Record provides:
\- platform
\- normalized id
\- subject record
\- datetime record

We can extract these components from typical session identifiers, such as
folder names, regardless of component order or presence of additional dates/times:
\>>> a = AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35_sorted_2023-12-16_06-52-11')
\>>> b = AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35')
\>>> c = AINDSessionRecord('sorted_2023-12-16_06-52-11_ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35_sorted_2023-12-16_06-52-11')
\>>> c
'ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35'
\>>> a == b == c
True

Components are also available for use:
\>>> a.platform, a.subject, a.date, a.time
('ecephys', 366122, '2022-04-25', '14:24:35')
\>>> a.date.year, a.date.month, a.date.day
(2022, 4, 25)
\>>> a.time.hour, a.time.minute, a.time.second
(14, 24, 35)
\>>> a.dt
datetime.datetime(2022, 4, 25, 14, 24, 35)
\>>> a.datetime
'2022-04-25 14:24:35'

Subject and date are validated on init:
\- subject must be a recent or near-future labtracks MID:
\>>> AINDSessionRecord('1_2022-04-25')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: AINDSessionRecord.id must be in format \<subject_id>\_\<date>\_\<idx \[optional\]>

- date must be a valid recent or near-future date:

```pycon
>>> AINDSessionRecord('366122_2022-13-25')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: AINDSessionRecord.id must be in format <platform>_<subject_id>_<date>_<time>
```

Comparisons are based on the session's normalized id:
\>>> assert AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') == AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35')
\>>> assert AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') == AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35_sorted_2023-12-16_06-52-11')
\>>> assert AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') == 'ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35'

Validator can also be used to check if a string is a valid session id:
\>>> AINDSessionRecord.validate_id('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') == None
True
\>>> AINDSessionRecord.validate_id('366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35') == None
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: AINDSessionRecord.id must match AINDSessionRecord.valid_id_regex

```pycon
>>> a = AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35')
>>> assert a[:] in a.id, f"slicing should access {a.id[:]=} , not the original value passed to init {a[:]=}"
```

\# hashes match string
\>>> assert 'ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35' in {AINDSessionRecord('ecephys_366122_2022-04-25_14-24-35')}

classes.name: npc_session.records.RigRecord

methods.name: npc_session.records.RigRecord.parse_id
documentation:
Pre-validation. Handle any parsing or casting to get to the stored
type.

To uniquely define a rig we need:
\- `id`: a multipart str corresponding to:

> \<id_major>.\<id_minor>-\<computer_index>
>
> where:
> \- `NP`, `NSB`, `SAM` are the major id components
> \- `id_minor` is a number for `NP` rigs or a supported cluster letter
> \- `computer_index` and the preceeding `-` aren't used for `NP` rigs, if
> missing for cluster rigs, it is set to `"UNKNOWN"`

Record provides:
\- normalized id
\- rig room number
\- ability to format as `aind_data_schema.core.rig.Rig.rig_id`

Normalize ids:
\>>> RigRecord('NP0')
'NP.0'
\>>> RigRecord('BEH.B')
'BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN'
\>>> RigRecord('BEH.D-Box1')
'BEHNSB.D-1'

Validate invalid rig ids:
\>>> RigRecord('BEH.D-Box7')
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: RigRecord.id must match ^((BEHNSB.(?:\[AC-Z\])-(?:\[1-6\]|UNKNOWN))|(BEHDEV.B-(?:\[1-6\]|UNKNOWN))|(NP.(?:\[0-3\])))\$, not 'BEHNSB.D-7'

Convert to `aind_data_schema.core.rig.Rig.rig_id`:
\>>> r = RigRecord('NSB.F-2')
\>>> r.behavior_cluster_id
'F'
\>>> r.is_behavior_cluster_rig
True
\>>> r.as_aind_data_schema_rig_id("346", datetime.date(2024, 4, 1))
'346_BEHNSB.F-2_20240401'

Update the modification date of `aind_data_schema.core.rig.Rig.rig_id`:
\>>> rig_id = '346_SAM.B-UNKNOWN_20240401'
\>>> RigRecord(rig_id).as_aind_data_schema_rig_id("342", datetime.date(2024, 4, 4))
'342_BEHDEV.B-UNKNOWN_20240404'
